## Figure Type

This is a **data inventory / register table** — specifically a numbered list (inventory or описание/опись) of geological documents, likely from a report archive or accompanying document register. It is not a map, log, or cross-section, but rather a bibliographic/administrative table cataloguing individual borehole geological cross-sections (геологические разрезы по скважинам).

---

## Coverage and Subject

The table catalogues **geological cross-sections (геологический разрез) for boreholes numbered 163–170 and 387–399**, corresponding to list entries №19–39 (with internal document numbers 82–102). All items are of the same document type: individual borehole geological sections at a scale of **1:200**. The boreholes span a sequential range suggesting they belong to a single drilling campaign within the survey area of Report 36572.

---

## Key Content

The table has **7 columns** (numbered 1–7 across the top), structured as follows:

| Col | Content |
|-----|---------|
| 1 | Sequential entry number (19–39), each marked with a checkmark (√) indicating the document has been verified or is present |
| 2 | Internal document/sheet number (82–102) |
| 3 | Document title: "Геологический разрез по скважине №___" (Geological cross-section for borehole No. ___) |
| 4 | Scale: **1:200** (stated explicitly for entry 19; all subsequent entries use **—"—** meaning "ditto/same") |
| 5 | Volume/part number: **I** (same for all) |
| 6 | Security classification: **не секретно** ("not secret/unclassified") — same for all |
| 7 | Document type/format: **светочувствительная** ("photosensitive" — i.e., blueprints or diazo/ozalid copies) — same for all |

All 21 entries (boreholes 163–170 and 387–399) are **uniform** in scale, classification, and reproduction format. The checkmarks (√) in column 1 indicate all documents have been accounted for or received.

## Research Value

This table is an **archival inventory page** confirming the existence and physical format of 21 individual borehole geological cross-sections, all drawn at 1:200 scale and reproduced as photosensitive (blueprint/ozalid) copies. For a research assistant, it identifies which specific borehole logs exist in the archive and their document numbers (82–102), enabling retrieval of the actual cross-sections for boreholes 163–170 and 387–399. The gap in borehole numbering (170 to 387) hints at a **two-phase or spatially separated drilling program**, which is significant for understanding the spatial distribution of subsurface data and mineral exploration coverage across the survey area.
